title: Gang of Four Design Patterns
description: Essential GoF design patterns for software engineers.

commands.description: Create an instance of a class with a method rather than a constructor.
commands.scenario: Create an instance of a class with a method rather than a constructor.

commands.description: Separate the construction of a complex object from its representation.
commands.scenario: Separate the construction of a complex object from its representation.

commands.description: Create families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes.
commands.scenario: Create families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes.

commands.description: Provide a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it.
commands.scenario: Provide a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it.

commands.description: Compose objects into tree structures to represent part-whole hierarchies.
commands.scenario: Compose objects into tree structures to represent part-whole hierarchies.

commands.description: Attach additional responsibilities to an object dynamically.
commands.scenario: Attach additional responsibilities to an object dynamically.

commands.description: Define a family of algorithms, encapsulate each one, and make them interchangeable.
commands.scenario: Define a family of algorithms, encapsulate each one, and make them interchangeable.

commands.description: Define an object that encapsulates how a set of objects interact.
commands.scenario: Define an object that encapsulates how a set of objects interact.

commands.description: Define a one-to-many dependency between objects so that when one object changes state, all its dependents are notified.
commands.scenario: Define a one-to-many dependency between objects so that when one object changes state, all its dependents are notified.
